comment: The procedural draft: the noise-driven base world Terrain.height() builds UNDER the blessed Strata tile (which replaces it where present). Valley's archipelago, moved out of game/world/terrain.gd's VALLEY_PATH/WALL_HEIGHT/SEABED consts and hardcoded noise seeds per FW4. A content-empty game ships no landform and falls back to terrain.gd's neutral defaults (no home valley, flat walls, generic noise). The amplitude 'profile' block below carries the draft's SHAPE constants (floor/wall/range/seabed multipliers + region shape) — data-driven so 800m alps or 5m dunes need no recompile; its DEFAULTS in terrain.gd/the kernel are exactly these values, so an absent block reproduces today's world. The per-game valley/seabed/noise parameters ride the kernel's existing set_base/set_home boundary.

profile.comment: Valley's amplitude profile — the historical hardcoded shape, now data. floor: base rolling ground; wall: valley-wall hill gain; range: mountain amplitude + [in,out] envelope (m); seabed: archipelago-floor texture; mesa_blend: mesa terrace lerp (0..1); volcano_power: volcanic flank concavity.

valley.comment: Home valley centerline (behind spawn, past the pond, to the shrine); floor stays low and dense, walls rise into an enclosing ridge plateau.
